product_overview: SessionFlow is a desktop and cloud-native system to manage instructor groups, scheduled teaching sessions, attendance, and real-time team chat. The platform exposes REST APIs for CRUD operations and a SignalR hub for real-time presence and chat.
core_goals: Provide secure engineer authentication and admin-controlled registration/approval flow.
Enable engineers to create and manage groups, timetables, and students with strict schedule validation.
Support full session lifecycle (create, start, end, cancel) and real-time attendance updates via SignalR.
Offer admin-only operations for platform configuration and engineer management.
Ensure robust real-time presence and group chat with graceful degradation when optional infra (Redis) is missing.

features.name: Authentication
features.description: Sign in engineers, register new engineers (creates pending account requiring admin approval), and fetch current authenticated user context.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login with body { Identifier: "engineer@example.com", Password: "Secret123!" } -> Receive 200 with { token: <JWT>, user: { ... } } -> GET /api/auth/me with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> -> Receive 200 with user profile

POST /api/auth/register with body { Name: "New Eng", Email: "new@eng.local", Password: "EngPass1!" } -> Receive 201 with { message: string, engineer: { id: string }, status: "Pending" } -> POST /api/auth/login with same credentials -> Receive 401 Invalid credentials (account pending admin approval)
POST /api/auth/login with body { Identifier: "bad@eng.local", Password: "wrong" } -> Receive 401 Invalid credentials

features.name: Group Management
features.description: Create, list, update and soft-delete student groups. Group creation strictly enforces Level and Frequency constraints and requires Schedules array length exactly equal to Frequency.
user_flows: POST /api/groups with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> and body { Name: "Level 2 - Mon+Wed", Description: "Afternoon cohort", Level: 2, Frequency: 2, NumberOfStudents: 20, StartingSessionNumber: 1, TotalSessions: 12, Schedules: [{ DayOfWeek: 1, StartTime: "14:00:00", DurationMinutes: 60 }, { DayOfWeek: 3, StartTime: "14:00:00", DurationMinutes: 60 }] } -> Receive 201 with { id: string, name: string } -> GET /api/groups with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> -> Receive 200 with Group[] including created group

PUT /api/groups/{id} with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> and body { Name: "Updated name", Description: "Updated" } -> Receive 200 with updated group -> DELETE /api/groups/{id} with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> -> Receive 200 { message: string }

POST /api/groups with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> and body { Name: "Invalid schedule", Level: 1, Frequency: 2, Schedules: [{ DayOfWeek: 2, StartTime: "10:00:00", DurationMinutes: 60 }] } -> Receive 400 Validation error (Schedules array count must match Frequency)
POST /api/groups without Authorization header -> Receive 401 Unauthorized

features.name: Session Management
features.description: Create scheduled sessions tied to groups and update session attendance. Support listing sessions for the authenticated engineer.
user_flows: POST /api/sessions with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> and body { GroupId: "<group-id>", ScheduledAt: "2026-04-10T09:00:00Z" } -> Receive 201 { id: string } -> GET /api/sessions with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> -> Receive 200 with Session[] including created session

PUT /api/sessions/{id}/attendance with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> and body [{ StudentId: "<student-id>", Status: "Present" }, { StudentId: "<student-id-2>", Status: "Late" }] -> Receive 200 Updated attendance
POST /api/sessions with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> and body missing GroupId or ScheduledAt -> Receive 400 Validation error
PUT /api/sessions/{invalid-id}/attendance with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> and body [...] -> Receive 404 Session not found

features.name: Student Management
features.description: Add students into groups and list students. Group capacity is enforced on add operations.
user_flows: POST /api/groups/{id}/students with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> and body { Name: "Student One" } -> Receive 201 { id: string, name: string, uniqueStudentCode: string } -> GET /api/students with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> -> Receive 200 Student[] including new student
POST /api/groups/{id}/students with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> and body { Name: "Extra Student" } when group has reached NumberOfStudents -> Receive 400 Group is full
POST /api/groups/{id}/students without Authorization header -> Receive 401 Unauthorized

features.name: Admin Operations
features.description: Admin-only endpoints to list engineers and read platform settings, used to approve registrations and configure system-wide data.
user_flows: GET /api/engineers with Authorization: Bearer <ADMIN_JWT> -> Receive 200 Engineer[]
GET /api/settings with Authorization: Bearer <ADMIN_JWT> -> Receive 200 Settings object
GET /api/engineers with Authorization: Bearer <NON_ADMIN_JWT> -> Receive 403 Forbidden
GET /api/settings without Authorization header -> Receive 401 Unauthorized

features.name: Real-time Presence & Team Chat (SignalR)
features.description: Establish SignalR connections using the JWT as an access_token query parameter to broadcast presence and chat events in real time (e.g., NewChatMessage, PresenceChanged).
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login with valid credentials -> Receive 200 with { token: <JWT> } -> CONNECT ws://localhost:5180/hub?access_token=<JWT> -> Receive connection established (101) -> Invoke hub method SendChatMessage with payload { GroupId: "<group-id>", Message: "Hello" } -> Receive broadcast event NewChatMessage on hub
CONNECT ws://localhost:5180/hub without access_token -> Receive 401 Unauthorized (connection rejected) -> Attempt to invoke hub methods -> No invocation accepted

features.name: Authentication
features.description: User login, engineer registration, and session management with JWT tokens

endpoints.description: Authenticate user with email and password, returns JWT token
Register a new engineer (returns pending status awaiting admin approval)

response_schema.201: { message: string, engineer: { id: string }, status: string }

features.name: Group Management
features.description: CRUD operations for student groups with strict schedule validation

endpoints.description: Create a new group with schedules. Frequency must be 1-3, Level must be 1-4, Schedules array count must exactly match Frequency.
List all groups for the authenticated engineer

features.name: Session Management
features.description: Create, start, end, and cancel teaching sessions tied to groups

endpoints.description: Create a new session for a group
List sessions for the authenticated engineer

features.name: Student Management
features.description: Add and manage students within groups

endpoints.description: Add a student to a group

response_schema.201: { id: string, name: string, uniqueStudentCode: string }

features.name: Admin Operations
features.description: Admin-only endpoints for engineer approval, settings, and codes

known_limitations.issue: Registration creates a PendingEngineer requiring admin approval before login is possible
known_limitations.impact: Newly registered engineers cannot authenticate until approved by admin

known_limitations.issue: Group creation strictly enforces Schedules array count must match Frequency value
known_limitations.impact: Automated tests must provide mathematically correct schedule arrays
